item: "Investigated follow-up session f5b4e148 that Sean flagged as still inefficient on click tooling despite the skill patch. Pulled the transcript and found the real pattern: `extract` WAS used on the parent subtree (good — the promotion worked), but without `--full`, so only goals came back and Coco had to fall back to `show` on two concluded children to recover the decision content. The anti-pattern wasn't 'avoid extract,' it was 'use extract without --full when the question is precisely about conclusions.' Per Sean's direction, patched the `click-extract` CLI tool in the framework (packages/plugins/ratchet/src/tools/click-extract.ts) to remove the --full flag entirely and always pass full=true — no opt-out for now. Library API (ratchet.extract) unchanged, still defaults full=false. Updated the obsolete goals-only test in ratchet.test.ts to assert always-includes-conclusions instead. Updated the CLI tool table in ratchet/README.md and the extract usage line in .claude/skills/clicks/SKILL.md to drop --full references. Verified: `nsg click extract c-mo2c3bs8` now returns conclusions inline without a flag. Framework commit 99ebd7c (rebased and pushed as 8ed3c17 after pulling remote). Also investigated the separate `nsg writ show` silent-output issue Sean flagged — root cause is that writ-show.ts calls clerk.show(params.id) directly with no short-id resolution (unlike all click tools which call ratchet.resolveId). Coco's flailing made it worse by using `2>/dev/null` which masked the 'Writ not found' error. Not patched yet — surfacing to Sean first."
    commissionable: false
    justification: sanctum

  - session: 3d3628f9-a6f8-4a13-9ce6-e2e111971e01
    date: 2026-04-17
    item: "Patched short-id resolution across all writ-* CLI tools in the framework (nexus commit 9da1e70). Added ClerkApi.resolveId mirroring ratchet.resolveId — exact-match fast path, LIKE prefix scan with ambiguity detection — and wired writ-show, writ-edit, writ-complete, writ-fail, writ-cancel, writ-publish, writ-link, writ-unlink, piece-add, and commission-post to resolve ids before hitting the underlying API. Added 4 resolveId() unit tests (exact match, prefix resolution, no match, ambiguous prefix) and updated the piece-add 'rejects when parent mandate does not exist' test regex since the error now surfaces from resolveId ('No writ found matching prefix') instead of post(). Smoke-verified `nsg writ show w-mo2xi6pt` now returns the writ by short id. Also updated .claude/agents/coco.md with a directive against reflexive `2>/dev/null` usage — the silent-output behavior that triggered Coco's flailing in f5b4e148 came from masking nsg's stderr errors; noise-filtering should use stdout pipes (| head, | grep) instead."
    commissionable: false
    justification: sanctum

  - session: 036f6916-d169-4cd0-851a-7e0e7306a73c
    date: 2026-04-17
    item: "Design session on the spider.follows meaning for load-bearing writ links. Locked the meaning name (spider.follows, after bikesheds through precedence-gate/dependency/precedence/sequence) and description (pure temporal-ordering primitive with per-consumer policy on individual terminal states). Locked Spider's failure policy (transition dependent to `stuck` with stuck-reason naming blocker) over silent-undispatchable and cascade-fail alternatives. Packaged the meaning declaration into the c-mo2e88aw Spider-wiring click. Concluded c-mo2e87dy (then superseded by refined name/description in a child lock click). Spun c-mo2z00fa for retry-ergonomics as a follow-up (Sean leans toward consumer-side chain-walking of `retries` links over a Clerk-side retry primitive). Spun six child clicks under c-mo2e88aw to decompose everything still needed for brief-readiness (c-mo2zim46 name/description lock [concluded], c-mo2zimyl cycle detection, c-mo2zinwa re-evaluation trigger, c-mo2ziowh auto-unstick on retry, c-mo2zipne conjunctive gating, c-mo2ziqig stuck-reason format). Notable session moments: Sean's correction that 'terminal state' conflated gate-release with success-required (forced separation); his reframe of the description to delegate per-state policy to consumers; his correction that Clockworks/standing-orders aren't implemented yet (my reflexive assumption). Also caught a Coco failure mode: stale state assumption on substrate brief status (thought it was pending; had actually completed ~10 hours earlier). Pure design work — no code changes. Wrap-up commit includes this session's notes + this log entry."
    commissionable: false
    justification: sanctum

GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Coco GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=coco@nexus.local \
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
coco/skill: keep click operations low-profile

Clicks were reading as foreground ceremony instead of background
bookkeeping — Sean was getting permission-requests, narrated completions,
IDs dropped through prose, mid-work discussions of click mechanics, and
sprawling essay-style conclusions. Fold five directives into durable
instructions so the behavior persists across sessions:

coco.md — new 'Keep click operations low-profile' subsection covering
chat behavior: no permission requests for routine ops, no bookkeeping
